data.description:
## Problem
`rebar explain plan` / `explain review` (and `explain <criterion-id>`) read their guide
markdown from `config.repo_root(...)/docs/...`. `repo_root` resolves to the *consuming
project's* git toplevel (or cwd), never the rebar install. So when rebar is installed as a
library/CLI/MCP and run against another project, the `docs/` tree is absent and every
`explain` guide lookup fails with missing-file. A client hit exactly this ("the author guide
lives in the rebar source repo, not this project").

## Goal
Make the plan-authoring guide (writing-a-passing-plan.md) and code-authoring guide
(passing-code-review.md) accessible through rebar's CLI, MCP, and Python library surfaces from
any installation, matching the established OSS convention for runtime-needed data files:
canonical copy lives *inside* the import package, read via `importlib.resources` (same pattern
rebar already uses for `criteria_routing.json`).

## Approach
1. Move the two prose guides to `src/rebar/_guides/{writing-a-passing-plan,passing-code-review}.md`
   (canonical, in-package) + add `src/rebar/_guides/__init__.py`. Hatchling ships all files
   under `src/rebar`, so they land in the wheel automatically.
2. `explain_guide(name)` resolves via `resources.files("rebar._guides") / relname` — repo-root
   independent. `AUTHOR_GUIDES` maps name -> filename.
3. `explain_criterion(id)` renders the section directly from the packaged registry via the
   existing `_guide_section_body` (same content the generated doc is built from) instead of
   reading `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` — so it is installable too. Overlay project
   criteria still resolve via `load_criteria(repo_root=...)`.
4. Leave `docs/{writing-a-passing-plan,passing-code-review}.md` as thin pointers to the
   in-package canonical source so existing links (docs/README.md, AGENTS.md) resolve.
5. Keep the dev/CI machinery (`regenerate_criteria_guide`, `validate_criteria_guide`,
   `_guide_path`, coach deep-links to the docs anchor) unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria
- `rebar explain plan`, `rebar explain review`, and `rebar explain <criterion-id>` all succeed
  with `REBAR_ROOT` pointed at a directory that has no `docs/` (installability guarantee).
- The MCP `explain_criterion` tool and the library `registry.explain_guide` /
  `registry.explain_criterion` succeed under the same repo-root-independent condition.
- The two guides are present as packaged resources (`resources.files("rebar._guides")`).
- Existing `docs/` links to the two guides still resolve (pointer files).
- lint + typecheck + the explain test suite pass; tests updated to assert repo-root
  independence instead of the old "missing-file when repo has no docs" behavior.
data.title: explain guides accessible from any install (package the author guides)
